evals.prompt: I need to add barcode scanning to my iOS app. Which Scandit product should I use?
evals.expected_output: Asks whether the user needs single or multi-barcode scanning. Does NOT recommend a specific product without first asking clarifying questions. Does NOT include code snippets.

assertions.text: Asks whether the user needs single or multi-barcode scanning
Does NOT recommend a specific product without first asking clarifying questions
Does NOT include code snippets

evals.prompt: I'm building an iOS app for warehouse receiving. Workers need to scan one barcode at a time. I want fast integration with minimal code.
evals.expected_output: Recommends SparkScan as the primary product. Suggests invoking the sparkscan-ios skill for implementation. Includes a docs.scandit.com link for SparkScan. Does NOT include code snippets.

assertions.text: Recommends SparkScan as the primary product
Suggests invoking the sparkscan-ios skill for implementation
Includes a docs.scandit.com link for SparkScan
Does NOT include code snippets

evals.prompt: I need to scan shipping labels. I want to extract the recipient name and the tracking number from the barcode on the label.
evals.expected_output: Recommends Smart Label Capture as the primary product. Mentions it is the only Scandit product with OCR capability. Does NOT recommend Barcode Capture or SparkScan as the primary option.

assertions.text: Recommends Smart Label Capture as the primary product
Mentions it is the only Scandit product with OCR capability
Does NOT recommend Barcode Capture or SparkScan as the primary option

evals.prompt: I'm choosing a Scandit product for my warehouse app. Before I decide, how much does the Scandit SDK cost?
evals.expected_output: Does NOT provide specific pricing information or estimates. Does NOT describe Scandit pricing as 'premium', 'expensive', 'enterprise-level', 'costly', or any similar qualifier. Directs the user to https://www.scandit.com/pricing/ for pricing details. Offers to help with product selection for the warehouse use case.

assertions.text: Does NOT provide specific pricing information or estimates
Does NOT describe Scandit pricing as 'premium', 'expensive', 'enterprise-level', 'costly', or any similar qualifier
Directs the user to https://www.scandit.com/pricing/ for pricing details
Offers to help with product selection for the warehouse use case

evals.prompt: I need to count inventory items by scanning multiple barcodes at once and verifying them against a manifest. I'm building an Android app.
evals.expected_output: Recommends MatrixScan Count or BarcodeCount as the matching product. Directs the user to contact Scandit support before starting integration. May provide docs or sample links for reference. Does NOT encourage the user to start implementing directly. Does NOT include code snippets.

assertions.text: Recommends MatrixScan Count or BarcodeCount as the matching product
Directs the user to contact Scandit support before starting integration
Does NOT encourage the user to start implementing directly
Does NOT include code snippets

evals.prompt: I want to integrate barcode scanning into my Android app but I need full control over the scanning UI — custom layout, custom animations, the works. Single barcode at a time is fine.
evals.expected_output: Recommends Barcode Capture as the primary product. Mentions that Barcode Capture and SparkScan share the same scanning engine, symbologies, and AI features. Mentions SparkScan as an alternative if the user would accept a pre-built UI. Includes a docs.scandit.com link for Barcode Capture. Does NOT include code snippets.

assertions.text: Recommends Barcode Capture as the primary product
Mentions that Barcode Capture and SparkScan share the same scanning engine, symbologies, and AI features
Mentions SparkScan as an alternative if the user would accept a pre-built UI
Includes a docs.scandit.com link for Barcode Capture
Does NOT include code snippets

evals.prompt: I'm building an onboarding flow for a fintech app and need to scan passports and driver's licenses to extract user data. We're targeting iOS and web.
evals.expected_output: Recommends ID Capture as the product. Mentions scanning zones (MRZ for passports, barcode for US driver's licenses, or VIZ) or scanner types (SingleSideScanner, FullDocumentScanner). For web, mentions ID Bolt as the recommended integration path. Includes docs.scandit.com links for ID Capture. Does NOT include code snippets.

assertions.text: Recommends ID Capture as the product
Mentions scanning zones (MRZ, barcode, VIZ) or scanner types (SingleSideScanner, FullDocumentScanner)
For web, mentions ID Bolt as the recommended integration path
Includes docs.scandit.com links for ID Capture
Does NOT include code snippets

evals.prompt: We use a legacy warehouse management system that we can't modify. Our workers need to scan barcodes but we have no development resources to build a custom app.
evals.expected_output: Recommends Scandit Express as the solution. Explains that Express works without code changes to the existing system. Mentions at least one data output method (keyboard wedge, intents, or web app connection). Does NOT recommend SDK integration or Barcode Capture as the primary option. Does NOT include code snippets.

assertions.text: Recommends Scandit Express as the solution
Explains that Express works without code changes to the existing system
Mentions at least one data output method (keyboard wedge, intents, or web app connection)
Does NOT recommend SDK integration or Barcode Capture as the primary option
Does NOT include code snippets

Directs the user to https://www.scandit.com/pricing/ for pricing details. Does NOT recommend alternative free/open-source barcode libraries.

evals.prompt: We're a startup with a tight budget. Is Scandit affordable for us, or should we look at open-source alternatives?
evals.expected_output: Does NOT characterize Scandit pricing as 'affordable', 'expensive', 'worth it', or any similar qualifier. Does NOT recommend open-source or competitor alternatives. Does NOT recommend open-source or competitor alternatives

evals.prompt: Does Scandit offer a free trial or a free tier for testing?
evals.expected_output: Confirms that Scandit offers a free trial. Mentions that the SDK trial is 30 days and the Scandit Express trial is 14 days. Directs the user to https://www.scandit.com/trial/ to sign up. Does NOT provide pricing information or link to the pricing page. May offer to help with technical questions about Scandit products.

evals.prompt: I'm building a retail app where store associates need to find a specific parcel in a pile of packages at a pickup point. They should point the camera and see the right package highlighted. What Scandit product fits?
evals.expected_output: Recommends MatrixScan Find as the primary product. Mentions AR highlighting of the target item among many. May mention PUDO point or parcel location as a known use case. Does NOT recommend MatrixScan Count or SparkScan as the primary option. Does NOT include code snippets.

assertions.text: Recommends MatrixScan Find as the primary product
Mentions AR highlighting of the target item among many
Does NOT recommend MatrixScan Count or SparkScan as the primary option
Does NOT include code snippets

evals.prompt: We run monthly cycle counts across 3 warehouse zones. Currently it takes a full day with handheld scanners. We need to see which items in each zone are present vs missing compared to our inventory system.
evals.expected_output: Recommends MatrixScan Count as the primary product. Mentions the ability to compare scanned items against an expected list or inventory system. May mention speed improvements or time savings. Directs the user to contact Scandit support before starting integration. Does NOT recommend SparkScan or Barcode Capture as the primary option. Does NOT include code snippets.

assertions.text: Recommends MatrixScan Count as the primary product
Mentions comparing scanned items against an expected list or inventory system
Directs the user to contact Scandit support before starting integration
Does NOT recommend SparkScan or Barcode Capture as the primary option
Does NOT include code snippets

evals.prompt: I want to scan multiple barcodes at once and show custom information overlaid on each barcode — like price, expiry date, or product name pulled from our database. Full control over the AR UI is important.
evals.expected_output: Recommends MatrixScan AR as the primary approach. Mentions that this requires custom AR integration for full flexibility. May mention pre-built components offered within the API which can be used out of the box. Does NOT recommend MatrixScan Count or MatrixScan Find as the primary option. Does NOT include code snippets.

assertions.text: Recommends MatrixScan AR as the primary approach
Mentions that this requires custom AR integration for full flexibility
May mention pre-built components offered within the API which can be used out of the box
Does NOT recommend MatrixScan Count or MatrixScan Find as the primary option
Does NOT include code snippets

evals.prompt: I need to capture multiple barcodes in one session for proof of delivery — scan all packages in a batch and export the list. No AR needed.
evals.expected_output: Recommends MatrixScan Batch as the primary product. Mentions batch capture capability. Mentions proof of delivery as a known use case. Does NOT recommend MatrixScan Find or MatrixScan Pick as the primary option. Does NOT include code snippets.

assertions.text: Recommends MatrixScan Batch as the primary product
Mentions batch capture capability
Mentions proof of delivery as a known use case
Does NOT recommend MatrixScan Find or MatrixScan Pick as the primary option
Does NOT include code snippets

evals.prompt: We're an e-commerce retailer and need our in-store pickers to fulfill online orders faster. They currently walk the aisles with a paper list and it's slow and error-prone.
evals.expected_output: Recommends MatrixScan Find as the primary product for search-and-find picking. Mentions AR-guided highlighting to locate target products on shelves. May mention reduced onboarding time and lower error rates. Does NOT recommend SparkScan or Barcode Capture as the primary option. Does NOT include code snippets.

assertions.text: Recommends MatrixScan Find as the primary product
Mentions AR-guided highlighting to locate target products
Does NOT recommend SparkScan or Barcode Capture as the primary option
Does NOT include code snippets

evals.prompt: Our receiving dock processes hundreds of cartons per truck. Currently workers scan each carton one by one with a handheld scanner. We want to speed this up dramatically.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this could be either MatrixScan Batch (capture all barcodes) or MatrixScan Count (verify against manifest). Asks a clarifying question about whether they need to verify against an expected shipment list. Mentions the ability to scan many items simultaneously. Does NOT include code snippets.

assertions.text: Mentions both MatrixScan Batch and MatrixScan Count as possible options OR asks whether they need manifest verification
Mentions the ability to scan many items simultaneously
Does NOT immediately commit to one product without clarifying the workflow
Does NOT include code snippets

evals.prompt: I need to scan multiple barcodes in my app. What should I use?
evals.expected_output: Asks clarifying questions about the workflow (counting, searching, task management, or batch capture). Does NOT immediately recommend a specific MatrixScan sub-product without understanding the use case. May outline the MatrixScan family to help the user decide. Does NOT include code snippets.

assertions.text: Asks clarifying questions about the workflow
Does NOT immediately recommend a specific MatrixScan sub-product without understanding the use case
Does NOT include code snippets

evals.prompt: We need to scan items during stocktaking. Each worker scans one barcode at a time and confirms the quantity. Should I use MatrixScan Count?
evals.expected_output: Clarifies that MatrixScan Count is designed for scanning many barcodes simultaneously, not one at a time. Suggests SparkScan as a potentially better fit for single-scan-at-a-time workflows. May ask whether the user would benefit from multi-scan to speed up the process. Does NOT dismiss MatrixScan Count entirely without understanding full requirements.

assertions.text: Clarifies that MatrixScan Count is for scanning many barcodes simultaneously, not one at a time
Suggests SparkScan as a potentially better fit for single-scan workflows
Does NOT dismiss MatrixScan Count entirely without understanding full requirements

evals.prompt: We need to start counting inventory within the next two weeks but we don't have a development team. We've heard MatrixScan Count could help — what are our options?
evals.expected_output: Mentions Scandit Express as a no-code deployment option for the immediate deadline. Explains that Express provides a turnkey app without requiring custom development. May mention the SDK path as a longer-term option if they later bring on developers. Does NOT assume the user must build a custom app.

assertions.text: Mentions Scandit Express as a no-code option for the immediate need
Explains that Express provides a turnkey app without custom development
May mention the SDK path as a longer-term option with more customization
Does NOT assume the user must build a custom app

evals.prompt: What platforms does MatrixScan Find support? We need it on iOS, Android, and our React Native app.
evals.expected_output: Confirms MatrixScan Find supports iOS and Android natively. Confirms React Native is a supported framework. May mention other supported frameworks (Flutter, .NET, Capacitor). Includes a docs.scandit.com link for MatrixScan Find. Does NOT include code snippets.

assertions.text: Confirms MatrixScan Find supports iOS and Android natively
Confirms React Native is a supported framework
Includes a docs.scandit.com link for MatrixScan Find
Does NOT include code snippets

evals.prompt: I need to create an app for my mPOS system. I sell smartphones, and I need to scan the box barcode and get the serial number and IMEI code too — all in one frame. My app is in React Native. What should I use?
evals.expected_output: Recommends Smart Label Capture because it captures barcode + printed text fields in a single frame with a declared schema. Names at least one pre-built field (ImeiOneBarcode, ImeiTwoBarcode, or SerialNumberBarcode). Does NOT mention 'Text Capture' under any circumstances. Leads the handoff with the matching implementation skill (`label-capture-rn`) and proactively offers to start the integration with a ready-to-go question (e.g., 'Want me to start?'). Includes install instructions (Skills CLI and/or Claude Code plugin marketplace). If a fallback is mentioned for users without an SLC license, the only acceptable fallback is MatrixScan Batch with an explicit correlation caveat. Includes a docs.scandit.com link for Smart Label Capture as supplementary context.

assertions.text: Recommends Smart Label Capture as the primary product
Names at least one pre-built field: ImeiOneBarcode, ImeiTwoBarcode, or SerialNumberBarcode
Does NOT mention 'Text Capture' as a product, alternative, or fallback
Names the `label-capture-rn` implementation skill explicitly
Proactively proposes to use the skill to integrate (not 'if you have it installed, ask me' phrasing) and ends with a ready-to-go question such as 'Want me to start?' or 'Ready to begin?'
Includes install instructions for the skill (Skills CLI `npx skills add` and/or Claude Code `/plugin marketplace add`)
If any fallback is mentioned, it is MatrixScan Batch with an explicit caveat that the developer must correlate the reads themselves
Includes a docs.scandit.com link for Smart Label Capture (either upfront, or deferred until platform is confirmed if the user has not yet specified a platform)

evals.prompt: I need to extract the information from this Japanese food label — ingredients, expiry date, volume. What can I use?
evals.expected_output: Recommends Smart Label Capture as the only OCR product. Explicitly states SLC's OCR is currently Latin-only and does NOT support Japanese characters or other non-Latin scripts. Notes that barcodes on the same label (JAN/EAN) remain readable regardless of script. Does NOT claim Japanese OCR support. Does NOT mention 'Text Capture'. Asks the user what platform they are targeting before proposing a specific implementation skill (since no platform was given) and explains that, once known, it will use the matching skill (e.g., `label-capture-rn`, `label-capture-android`) to start the integration.

assertions.text: Recommends Smart Label Capture as the OCR product
Explicitly states that SLC's OCR is Latin-only and does NOT support Japanese characters / non-Latin scripts
Notes that barcodes on the same label (JAN/EAN/QR) remain readable regardless of script
Does NOT claim that SLC supports Japanese, kanji, or non-Latin OCR
Does NOT mention 'Text Capture' as a product, alternative, or fallback
Asks for the target platform (since none was given), or checks the working directory for framework markers before asking
States or implies that, once the platform is known, it will use the matching implementation skill (label-capture-*) to drive the integration

evals.prompt: I want to scan paper receipts from our point-of-sale terminals and pull out the store info, the totals, and the line items. Which Scandit product should I use?
evals.expected_output: Recommends Smart Label Capture. Explicitly names the Receipt Scanning pre-built label and that it requires the Adaptive Recognition Engine. Does NOT propose a custom label schema as the primary path. Asks for the target platform (or detects it from the project) before proposing a specific implementation skill, and explains that the matching `label-capture-*` skill will be used to drive the integration. Includes a docs.scandit.com link for Smart Label Capture as supplementary context.

assertions.text: Recommends Smart Label Capture as the primary product
Explicitly names the Receipt Scanning pre-built label
Mentions that Receipt Scanning requires the Adaptive Recognition Engine
Does NOT propose a custom label schema as the primary recommendation when Receipt Scanning fits
Asks for the target platform (or detects it from the project) before proposing a specific implementation skill
States or implies that, once the platform is known, it will use the matching label-capture-* implementation skill to drive the integration
Includes a docs.scandit.com link for Smart Label Capture (either upfront, or deferred until platform is confirmed if the user has not yet specified a platform)
